final_embedding_text:
Context: This chunk is from the Cushman & Wakefield MARKETBEAT BENGALURU RESIDENTIAL Q1 2025 report, providing an overview of market fundamentals and economic indicators for the residential sector in Bengaluru. It details current and historical unit launches, capital values, and rental trends, alongside a 12-month forecast for key indicators.

Chunk Content: # MARKETBEAT BENGALURU RESIDENTIAL Q1 2025

## MARKET FUNDAMENTALS

| Indicator | Value | YOY Chg | 12-Month Forecast |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| New unit launches | ~12200 | Increase | Increase |
| Prime CBD Capital Value (INR/SF) | 18-23K | Increase | Increase |
| NHB Residex (September 2024) | 152 | Increase | Increase |

*Source: NHB*

## ECONOMIC INDICATORS

| Indicator | Value | YOY Chg | 12-Month Forecast |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| GDP Growth (Q3FY24-25) | 6.20% | Decrease | Increase |
| CPI Inflation | 3.60% | Decrease | Increase |
| External Benchmark Lending Rate | 8.90% | Decrease | Decrease |

*Source: MOSPI, RBI, SBI*

---

### HIGHEST QUARTERLY LAUNCHES IN POST COVID PERIOD
Bengaluru recorded launch of around 12,200 units in Q1-25, a record figure in the post Covid period. This marks a growth rate of 38% on yoy basis and ~17% expansion as compared to previous quarter. In a deviation from trend witnessed over the past several quarters, East Bengaluru market recorded much lower launches in the quarter while that across the southern locations witnessed sharp increase. The south, southeast and southwest submarkets cumulatively accounted for around 54% of quarterly launches.

Locations such as Sarjapur Road, Bannerghatta Road and HSR Layout recorded large upper-mid and luxury project launches by branded, listed developers. Northern submarkets (north, northeast and northwest) contributed 38% of the launches in the quarter, including a large township project by a national, listed developer at Devanahalli in north Bengaluru. East submarket (Whitefield) contributed just 8% of the launches in Q1. The quarter also saw a luxury project launch in the CBD submarket.

### MID-SEGMENT SHARE RISES, ALTHOUGH HIGH-END REMAINS STRONG
The mid-segment category homes stood dominant at 60% in the overall launches, increasing its share from ~50% share on average in the last 8 quarters. However, the luxury & high-end segment share at 40% remains strong in Q1, with Southern submarkets recording robust quantum of unit launches within this category. There were no affordable launches in the quarter.

### MARGINAL APPRECIATION IN CAPITAL VALUES
Average city-wide capital values posted marginal growth of 1-2% on a quarterly basis and 8-9% on a yoy basis. East, south east and north submarkets witnessed appreciation in capital values. Average city-wide rentals remained unchanged on a quarterly basis.

---

### RESIDENTIAL UNIT LAUNCHES
| Period | Unit launches (Approx.) |
| :--- | :--- |
| 2020 Q1 | 4,500 |
| 2020 Q2 | 3,000 |
| 2020 Q3 | 3,500 |
| 2020 Q4 | 3,500 |
| 2021 Q1 | 4,000 |
| 2021 Q2 | 4,000 |
| 2021 Q3 | 4,000 |
| 2021 Q4 | 6,500 |
| 2022 Q1 | 7,500 |
| 2022 Q2 | 8,500 |
| 2022 Q3 | 9,500 |
| 2022 Q4 | 7,500 |
| 2023 Q1 | 8,000 |
| 2023 Q2 | 8,500 |
| 2023 Q3 | 8,500 |
| 2023 Q4 | 9,500 |
| 2024 Q1 | 9,000 |
| 2024 Q2 | 10,000 |
| 2024 Q3 | 10,500 |
| 2024 Q4 | 10,500 |
| Q1 2025 | 12,200 |

**Caption:** Residential Unit Launches. Y-axis: Unit launches (500 to 10,500). X-axis: 2020 to Q1 2025.

---

### CAPITAL VALUES/ RENTAL VALUES
| Year | Capital Values (INR/sf) | Rental Values (INR/m) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 2020 | ~6,500 | ~25,000 |
| 2021 | ~7,000 | ~30,000 |
| 2022 | ~7,500 | ~35,000 |
| 2023 | ~8,000 | ~40,000 |
| 2024 | ~8,800 | ~50,000 |
| Q1 2025 | ~9,000 | ~50,000 |

**Caption:** Capital Values/ Rental Values. Left Y-axis: Capital Values (INR/sf) (5,000 to 9,000). Right Y-axis: Rental Values (INR/m) (20,000 to 60,000). X-axis: 2020 to Q1 2025.

---
Better never settles
© 2025 Cushman & Wakefield

final_embedding_text:
Context: This chunk from the Cushman & Wakefield 'MARKETBEAT BENGALURU RESIDENTIAL Q1 2025' report provides detailed capital and rental values for various residential submarkets in Bengaluru as of Q1 2025. It also highlights key project launches and construction completions during the same quarter, along with short-term outlooks for values.

Chunk Content: # BENGALURU RESIDENTIAL Q1 2025

### CAPITAL VALUES AS OF Q1 2025

| SUBMARKET | AVERAGE QUOTED CAPITAL VALUE** (INR/SF) | QoQ CHANGE (%) | YoY CHANGE (%) | SHORT TERM OUTLOOK |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **High-end segment** | | | | |
| Central | 18,000-30,000 | 0% | 0% | Stable |
| South | 10,550 – 15,000 | 1% | 10% | Increase |
| East | 9,500-14,000 | 2% | 13% | Increase |
| North | 9,000-13,500 | 1% | 12% | Increase |
| **Mid segment** | | | | |
| Central | 9,500-15,000 | 0% | 5% | Stable |
| East | 6,500-8,500 | 2% | 9% | Increase |
| South-East | 6,800-8,000 | 1% | 8% | Increase |
| North | 6,300-7,800 | 2% | 8% | Increase |
| South | 7,000-9,000 | 0% | 5% | Stable |
| Off-Central I | 9,000-14,000 | 0% | 5% | Stable |
| Off-Central II | 7,800-10,000 | 0% | 6% | Stable |
| North-West | 6,500-8,000 | 1% | 6% | Increase |
| Far South | 5,000-6,500 | 1% | 4% | Stable |
| West | 5,200-6,700 | 0% | 4% | Stable |

### RENTAL VALUES AS OF Q1 2025

| SUBMARKET | AVERAGE QUOTED RENT (INR/MONTH) | QoQ CHANGE (%) | YoY CHANGE (%) | SHORT TERM OUTLOOK |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **High-end segment** | | | | |
| Central | 155,000-360,000 | 0% | 5% | Stable |
| South | 62,000-125,000 | 0% | 10% | Stable |
| East | 86,000-310,000 | 1% | 15% | Stable |
| North | 72,000-210,000 | 1% | 13% | Stable |
| **Mid segment** | | | | |
| Central | 80,000-120,000 | 0% | 10% | Stable |
| South-East | 35,000-52,000 | 1% | 11% | Increase |
| North-West | 28,000-39,000 | 0% | 8% | Stable |
| West | 24,000-31,000 | 0% | 6% | Stable |
| East | 35,000-48,000 | 1% | 13% | Increase |

### KEY PROJECTS LAUNCHED IN Q1 2025

| PROPERTY | LOCATION | DEVELOPER | UNITS (Nos.) | UNIT SIZE (SF) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Godrej Shettigere | Devanahalli | Godrej Properties | 4,000 | 900-1400 |
| Prestige Southern Star | Bannerghatta Road | Prestige Group | 2,130 | 690-2700 |
| Birla Evara | Sarjapur Road | Birla Estates | 1,600 | 474-1733 |

### KEY CONSTRUCTION COMPLETIONS IN Q1 2025

| PROPERTY | LOCATION | DEVELOPER | UNITS (Nos.) | UNIT SIZE (SF) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Sumadhura Aspire Amber | Whitefield | Sumadhura Group | 326 | 750 - 1250 |

---

Data collated from primary and secondary resources. Estimations are subject to change
* Rental and capital values have been depicted only for key submarkets
** Quoted capital value on carpet area based on agreement values which includes, Base Rate, Car Parking Charges, Internal Development Charges, etc.
The above values for high-end segment are for units typically of 2,000-4,000 sf
The above values for mid segment are for units typically of 1,600-2,000 sf
Affordable housing has been defined as units with a carpet area of 60 sq.mt in metros / 90 sq.mt in non-metros and value up to INR 45 lakhs
Data for the first quarter is based on market information collected until 15th March 2025

### KEY TO SUBMARKETS

**HIGH-END SEGMENT**
**Central:** Lavelle Road, Palace Cross Road, Off Cunningham Road, Ulsoor Road, Richmond Road, Sankey Road
**South:** Koramangala, Bannerghatta Road, JP Nagar, Banashankari
**Off Central:** Frazer town, Benson Town, Richards Town, Dollars Colony
**East:** Whitefield, Old Airport Road
**North:** Hebbal, Jakkur, Devanahalli
**North-west:** Malleshwaram, Rajajinagar, Yeshwanthpur

**MID SEGMENT**
**Central:** Brunton Road, Artillery Road, Ali Askar Road, Cunningham Road
**East:** Whitefield, Old Airport Road, Old Madras Road, Budigere Cross
**South-east:** Sarjapur Road, Outer Ring Road (Marathahalli- Sarjapur), HSR Layout, Hosur Road
**South:** Jayanagar, J P Nagar, Kanakapura Road, Bannerghatta Road, BTM Layout, Banashankari
**North:** Hebbal, Bellary Road, Yelahanka, Doddaballapur Road, Hennur Road, Thanisandra Road
**Off Central-I:** Vasanth Nagar, Richmond Town, Indiranagar
**Off Central-II:** Cox Town, Frazer Town, Benson Town, etc.
**North-west:** Malleshwaram, Rajajinagar, Tumkur Road
**Far South:** Electronic City, Attibele, Chandapur
**West:** Mysore Road, Uttarahalli Main Road, Magadi Road

---

**SWARNAVA ADHIKARY**
Assistant Vice President, Research
Tel: +91 80 40465555
swarnava.adhikary@cushwake.com

**SUVISHESH VALSAN**
Senior Director, Research
